businessDomainProposals.rationale: Essential for a platform that coordinates actions between multiple parties (homeowners, vendors, CAMs). Workflows like violation notices and service call updates depend on reliable communication. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Reporting & Analytics
businessDomainProposals.description: Provides dashboards, reports, and data visualizations to help users like Board Members, CAMs, and Service Providers understand performance and make informed decisions.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Addresses the stated pain points of Board Members and CAMs regarding lack of visibility and timely information. A core requirement for any management platform. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Document Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Provides a centralized and secure repository for storing, organizing, versioning, and sharing all documents across the platform.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Extends the simple 'upload' feature into a robust system needed for compliance, history, and AI processing. Many domains (Property, Compliance, Financials) rely on documents. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Architectural & Change Request Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages the process for homeowners to submit architectural or modification requests to the community association, and for the board/committee to review and approve them.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: This is a standard, major workflow in community association management that is currently missing. It's a key part of the 'governance' aspect of the platform. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: System Administration & Configuration
businessDomainProposals.description: Tools for Hestami Staff to manage the platform itself, including tenant (Community/Service Provider) onboarding, system-wide settings, feature flags, and monitoring.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Required for the 'Hestami Staff / Admin' persona to effectively manage the multi-tenant, multi-pillar platform as described in their goals and pain points. Source: ai-proposed

workflowProposals.name: Service Provider Recommendation
workflowProposals.description: When a homeowner submits a service request, this workflow uses AI to analyze the request and recommend the best-fit service providers from the network.
steps: A service request is created by a homeowner.
The system parses the request description, category, and attached media using natural language processing.
The AI model matches the request's needs against provider profiles, considering factors like skills, service area, past performance ratings, and current availability.
A ranked list of the top 3-5 providers is generated.
The recommended providers are presented to the homeowner.
triggers: Creation of a new service request.

workflowProposals.name: Vendor Credential Expiration Monitoring
workflowProposals.description: This workflow automatically monitors the expiration dates of vendor credentials (like insurance or licenses) and triggers alerts to ensure compliance.
steps: A nightly system job scans all vendor profiles for credentials expiring within the next 60 days.
If an expiring credential is found, a notification is sent to the Service Provider.
Follow-up notifications are sent at 30 days and 7 days before expiration.
If a credential expires, the system automatically flags the provider's profile as 'Non-Compliant' and temporarily suspends them from receiving new job requests.
A notification is sent to the Hestami Admin for review.
Once the provider uploads a new, valid credential, the system lifts the suspension.

workflowProposals.name: Violation Escalation Process
workflowProposals.description: Automates the escalation of unresolved community violations according to the association's predefined enforcement policy.
steps: A violation is created with a resolution deadline.
If the deadline passes and the violation is not marked as resolved, the system checks the community's enforcement policy.
The system automatically escalates the violation to the next stage (e.g., 'Second Notice', 'Final Notice').
A new notice is generated and sent to the homeowner.
If defined in the policy, a fine is automatically added to the homeowner's account ledger.
The workflow repeats until the violation is resolved or reaches the final stage (e.g., 'Sent to Legal').

workflowProposals.name: Automated Accounts Receivable (AR) Collections
workflowProposals.description: Manages the process of collecting delinquent homeowner assessments based on the community's collections policy.
steps: An assessment payment becomes overdue.
The system automatically applies a late fee to the homeowner's account after the grace period ends.
A 'Friendly Reminder' notice is sent to the homeowner.
If payment is not received after a set number of days (e.g., 30 days), a 'Formal Demand' letter is generated and sent.
The workflow continues to escalate based on the policy, potentially notifying the board, or flagging the account for legal action at the final stage.
All communications and fees are logged on the homeowner's account for a complete audit trail.
triggers: An assessment due date passing without payment.

workflowProposals.name: AI Document Analysis and Indexing
workflowProposals.description: When a document is uploaded, this workflow uses AI to process its content, extract key information, and make it searchable.
steps: A user uploads a document (e.g., PDF, JPG) to the system.
The document is sent to an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) service to convert any images of text into machine-readable text.
A Natural Language Processing (NLP) model analyzes the text to identify key entities (e.g., dates, names, invoice amounts, asset model numbers).
The extracted text and entities are added to a search index.
The document is auto-tagged with keywords (e.g., 'invoice', 'HVAC', 'warranty') for easier filtering and organization.
triggers: File upload to the document management system.

workflowProposals.name: Work Order Lifecycle Notifications
workflowProposals.description: Keeps all stakeholders informed about the status of a work order as it progresses from creation to completion.
steps: A work order is created and awarded to a Service Provider.
System notifies the Service Provider of the new job.
The Service Provider schedules the job and assigns a Technician.
System notifies the Homeowner of the scheduled service date.
The Technician marks themselves as 'En Route'.
System sends an 'On the way' notification to the Homeowner with an ETA.
The Technician completes the job.
System notifies the Homeowner and the Service Provider's back-office that the job is complete and ready for invoicing and rating.

integrationProposals.name: Payment Gateway
integrationProposals.description: Processes online payments from homeowners for assessments and invoices. Handles credit card and ACH transactions securely.
integrationProposals.rationale: Required for UJ-8 (Homeowner Pays Annual Community Assessment) and WF-4 (Automated AR Collections) to allow for electronic payments.

integrationProposals.name: Bank Account Synchronization
integrationProposals.description: Connects to community association bank accounts to automatically import transaction data for financial reconciliation within the DOM-ACCOUNTING module.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates the bank reconciliation process, reducing manual data entry for CAMs and ensuring the General Ledger is accurate and up-to-date.

integrationProposals.name: Accounting Software Sync
integrationProposals.description: Enables the export of financial data, including the General Ledger and financial reports, to external accounting platforms.
integrationProposals.rationale: Allows accounting professionals and management companies to continue using their preferred system of record for corporate-level accounting.

integrationProposals.name: Email Delivery Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends all transactional and bulk emails from the platform, including notifications, alerts, reports, and violation notices.
integrationProposals.rationale: Provides a reliable and scalable mechanism for all system communications (DOM-COMMS) and is essential for WF-6 (Work Order Lifecycle Notifications).

integrationProposals.name: SMS Messaging Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends SMS messages for time-sensitive alerts, such as new job assignments for technicians or urgent community announcements.
integrationProposals.rationale: Offers a direct and immediate communication channel for high-priority notifications within DOM-COMMS.

integrationProposals.name: Push Notification Service
integrationProposals.description: Delivers push notifications to the mobile applications used by Homeowners and Technicians.
integrationProposals.rationale: Enables real-time, on-device alerts for UJ-5 (Technician Completes a Field Service Job) and general homeowner notifications.

integrationProposals.name: Cloud File Storage
integrationProposals.description: Provides a secure, scalable, and durable location to store all documents, photos, and other binary files managed by DOM-DOCS.
integrationProposals.rationale: Required to handle the storage and versioning of all documents (ENT-DOCUMENT) uploaded to the system.

integrationProposals.name: Identity Provider (SSO)
integrationProposals.description: Allows users to authenticate using third-party identity providers, such as Google, Microsoft, or enterprise SSO solutions like Okta.
integrationProposals.rationale: Simplifies the login process for all user personas and allows enterprise customers (CAMs, Service Providers) to enforce their own security policies.

integrationProposals.name: AI/ML Platform
integrationProposals.description: Provides the machine learning models and infrastructure for document analysis (OCR, NLP), data extraction, and powering recommendation algorithms.
standardProviders: Google Cloud AI (Vertex AI, Document AI)
integrationProposals.rationale: Core component for WF-5 (AI Document Analysis) and WF-1 (Service Provider Recommendation) within the DOM-AI domain.

integrationProposals.name: Mapping & Geocoding Service
integrationProposals.description: Provides address validation, geocoding (lat/long lookup), map visualization, and route planning capabilities.
integrationProposals.rationale: Essential for DOM-PROPERTY to validate and display property addresses and for DOM-FIELD-OPS to enable route planning for technicians.

integrationProposals.name: Vendor Background Check Service
integrationProposals.description: Integrates with services that perform background checks and verify credentials (licenses, insurance) for service providers.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-2 (Vendor Credential Expiration Monitoring) and enhances trust and safety within the DOM-VENDOR network by automating credential validation.

integrationProposals.name: Public Records API
integrationProposals.description: Connects to databases to pull and validate property details, such as ownership, parcel information, and tax records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates the population of accurate property data during UJ-1 (Homeowner Onboards), reducing manual input and ensuring data integrity for DOM-PROPERTY.

integrationProposals.name: Physical Mail Automation Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends legally required notices and documents via postal mail, such as certified letters for severe violations or collections.
integrationProposals.rationale: Fulfills legal requirements for DOM-COMPLIANCE and DOM-ACCOUNTING where email is not sufficient for formal notices (e.g., WF-3, WF-4).

integrationProposals.name: eSignature Service
integrationProposals.description: Enables legally binding electronic signatures on documents, such as vendor contracts or architectural review approvals.
integrationProposals.rationale: Streamlines formal approval workflows like UJ-4 (CAM Manages an Architectural Request) and vendor onboarding within DOM-ARCH-REVIEW and DOM-VENDOR.

qualityAttributes: The system must enforce strict multi-tenancy to ensure complete data isolation and configuration separation for each Tenant (ENT-TENANT) organization.
Access to all system resources must be governed by a granular Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) model, as defined by User, Role, and Permission entities.
All sensitive data at rest, including documents (DOM-DOCS) and profile information (ENT-PROFILE), must be encrypted using AES-256 or a stronger algorithm.
All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
The platform must comply with relevant data privacy regulations, including GDPR and CCPA, regarding the handling of personal user data.
Any handling of payment card information must adhere to PCI-DSS standards, primarily by delegating processing to a compliant payment gateway.
The platform must be designed for high availability with a target uptime of 99.9%, supported by redundant infrastructure and automated failover.
The architecture must scale horizontally to accommodate growth in users, communities, and data volume without performance degradation.
Mobile application APIs must be optimized for low latency and intermittent connectivity to ensure a responsive experience for field technicians (P-6).
All create, update, and delete operations on critical entities (e.g., ENT-TRANSACTION, ENT-VIOLATION, ENT-USER_ROLE) must be recorded in an immutable audit log (ENT-AUDIT_LOG).
A robust disaster recovery plan must be in place, including regular automated backups and a tested restoration process, to ensure business continuity.
The AI document analysis pipeline (WF-5) must be scalable to handle bulk uploads and return results within a timeframe that does not disrupt user workflows.
The platform must expose a secure, versioned, and well-documented RESTful or GraphQL API to allow for third-party integrations.
All user-facing web and mobile interfaces must adhere to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards.

finalized_plan.title: Product Discovery for Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform
finalized_plan.summary: This plan outlines the comprehensive product vision, personas, user journeys, technical architecture, and phased implementation strategy for the Hestami AI Real Property OS. This integrated platform, composed of a Home Assistant, Field Services Management, and Community Association Management pillars, will serve property owners, service providers, and community associations. The system is designed to be a secure, scalable, multi-tenant solution leveraging an AI-driven approach to streamline property management, maintenance, and governance.

requirements.text: The platform must support AI-driven extraction, classification, and summarization of documents.
The system must have an auditable, compliant GL structure for accounting.
The platform must support automatic periodic assessment posting for community associations.
The platform must provide native iOS and Android mobile clients for homeowners and technicians with offline-first capabilities.

decisions.text: The technology stack is pre-defined, including SvelteKit for the frontend, Node.js (Bun) for the backend, PostgreSQL for the database, and DBOS for the workflow engine.
The product will be built in three phases, corresponding to the three pillars: 1. Home Assistant, 2. Field Services Management, 3. Community Association Management.
Pillar 2 (Field Services Management) will be modeled after ServiceTitan.
Pillar 3 (Community Association Management) will be modeled after Vantaca.

constraints.text: Cloudflare CDN will be the only public entry point to the system.
External integrations with partners like Nextdoor are out-of-scope for the initial implementation.

openQuestions.text: What are the specific feature priorities within Phase 1 (Home Real Property Assistant) to constitute a minimum viable product (MVP)?
How will the three pillars be integrated? For example, how will a service call from a homeowner in Pillar 1 interact with a service provider using Pillar 2?
What is the monetization strategy for each pillar? Will it be subscription-based, transactional, or a hybrid model?

finalized_plan.proposedApproach:
The Hestami platform will be developed as a modular, multi-tenant system deployed to a cloud environment and secured via Cloudflare as the single public entry point. The architecture will be built upon the decided technology stack, ensuring a robust and scalable solution.

**Technology Stack:**
- **Web Frontend:** SvelteKit with TypeScript for a modern, reactive user interface.
- **Backend:** Node.js with the Bun runtime, likely using a performant framework like Fastify, structured into logical modules based on the identified business domains.
- **Database:** PostgreSQL, leveraging its reliability and features like row-level security to enforce multi-tenancy.
- **Workflow Engine:** DBOS will be used to orchestrate complex, long-running, and durable business processes such as violation escalations, work order lifecycles, and credential monitoring.
- **Mobile:** Native iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) and Android (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose) applications will be developed to provide an optimal experience for homeowners and field technicians. These apps will be designed with an offline-first approach, using a local database that syncs with the backend.

**Core Architecture:**
- **Multi-Tenancy:** A strict multi-tenancy model will be implemented at the database and application layers, primarily using a `tenantId` to isolate data for each client organization (e.g., a Community Management Company).
- **API Layer:** A secure RESTful or GraphQL API will serve all client applications (web and mobile). Authentication and authorization will be managed via an integrated Identity Provider (e.g., Auth0, Okta) using JWTs and a Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) model.
- **AI Integration:** The 'AI & Data Intelligence' domain will act as a service layer abstracting communications with external AI platforms (e.g., Google AI, OpenAI). This will centralize logic for document analysis (OCR/NLP) and recommendation engines.

**Implementation Phasing:**
- **Phase 1 (Home Assistant):** Focus on the core homeowner experience. This involves building the foundational domains: `Identity Management`, `Property & Asset Management`, and `Document Management`. The initial `Service & Work Order Management` domain will be created to support service requests. The homeowner web portal will be developed to deliver User Journeys UJ-1 and UJ-2.
- **Phase 2 (Field Services):** Build the supply side of the marketplace. This requires expanding `Service & Work Order Management` and developing the `Field Service Operations` and `Vendor & Provider Management` domains. The native mobile apps for technicians (UJ-5) and the web portal for service providers (UJ-3) are the key deliverables.
- **Phase 3 (Community Management):** Develop the enterprise management pillar. This includes building the `Community & Compliance Management`, `Financials & Accounting`, and `Architectural & Change Request Management` domains. Web portals for CAMs and Board Members will be created to support UJ-4, UJ-6, UJ-7, UJ-8, and UJ-9. Critical integrations with payment gateways and banking services will be implemented.
finalized_plan.productVision: To create an integrated, holistic, end-to-end home services operating system powered by AI.
finalized_plan.productDescription: The Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform is a comprehensive ecosystem composed of three pillars: a Home Real Property Assistant for property owners, a Field Services Management platform for service providers, and a Community Association Management system for HOAs. It aims to streamline property management, maintenance, and governance by connecting all stakeholders on a single, intelligent platform.

personas.name: Homeowner / Resident
personas.description: An individual who owns or resides in a property. They are the primary user of the Home Real Property Assistant and a member of a community managed by the CAM pillar.
goals: Easily find and hire trusted service providers.
Manage maintenance and repairs with minimal effort.
Stay informed about their community and manage their obligations (e.g., pay assessments).
painPoints: Difficulty finding reliable contractors.
Inconsistent and confusing bids from vendors.
Time-consuming process of coordinating and managing service calls.

personas.name: Service Provider / Contractor
personas.description: A business that provides trade services (e.g., plumbing, HVAC, electrical). They are the primary user of the Field Services Management pillar.
goals: Efficiently manage their schedule, dispatch, and workforce.
Streamline quoting, invoicing, and payments.
Grow their business and improve customer satisfaction.
painPoints: Manual, paper-based processes for work orders and invoicing.
Inefficient scheduling and routing of technicians.
Lack of a centralized system to manage customer history and communication.

personas.name: Community Association Manager (CAM)
personas.description: A professional responsible for managing the daily operations of a homeowners association or community.
goals: Automate routine tasks like accounting, compliance, and communications.
Efficiently manage work orders, violations, and architectural requests.
Provide excellent service to the board and residents.
painPoints: Disparate systems for accounting, communication, and work order management.
Manual and time-consuming workflows.
Difficulty maintaining a complete and auditable record of all actions.

personas.name: Board Member
personas.description: An elected volunteer who governs the community association.
goals: Gain real-time visibility into the community's operations and financial health.
Make informed decisions based on accurate data.
Ensure compliance with governing documents and regulations.
painPoints: Lack of transparency and timely information from the management company.
Inefficient communication and decision-making processes.
Difficulty tracking the status of projects and initiatives.

personas.name: Hestami Staff / Admin
personas.description: An employee of Hestami who administers the platform and may assist users.
goals: Onboard and support users from all pillars.
Monitor the health and performance of the platform.
Facilitate interactions between different user groups (e.g., matching homeowners with service providers).
painPoints: Managing a complex, multi-sided platform.
Ensuring data privacy and security across different tenants.
Providing effective support to a diverse user base.

personas.name: Technician
personas.description: An individual field service worker employed by a Service Provider. They are the primary user of the mobile app for completing jobs, updating status, and communicating from the field.
goals: Get all job information in one place on my mobile device
Easily navigate to job sites
Quickly update job status and upload photos of completed work
Communicate with the back office and the customer
painPoints: Receiving incomplete or inaccurate work order information
Juggling paper-based work orders
Wasting time driving back to the office for parts or information
Difficulty documenting work performed and getting customer sign-off

userJourneys.title: Homeowner Onboards and Sets Up Their Property
userJourneys.scenario: A new user has just signed up for the Hestami Home Real Property Assistant. They need to create their account and populate it with their property's information to start managing it.

steps.action: Signs up for a new account using their email address and a secure password.
steps.expectedOutcome: Account is created and a verification email is sent.

steps.action: Sends a verification email to the user's provided address.
steps.expectedOutcome: Email is delivered.

steps.action: Clicks the verification link in the email, logs in, and completes their user profile.
steps.expectedOutcome: User is authenticated and their profile is saved.

steps.action: Navigates to the 'Properties' section and adds their primary residence, entering details like address, property type, and age.
steps.expectedOutcome: The property is created and saved in the system.

steps.action: Adds key assets to the property, such as an HVAC unit, water heater, and refrigerator, including details like brand, model, and installation date.
steps.expectedOutcome: Assets are associated with the property, creating a digital inventory.

steps.action: Uploads important documents like the home inspection report, appliance manuals, and receipts to the property's document repository.
steps.expectedOutcome: Documents are securely stored and linked to the relevant property or asset.

acceptanceCriteria: A homeowner can successfully create and verify an account.
A homeowner can add at least one property with detailed information.
A homeowner can add multiple assets and documents to their property.

userJourneys.title: Homeowner Requests a Service for a Home Repair
userJourneys.scenario: A homeowner discovers a leaky pipe under their kitchen sink and needs to find a qualified plumber to fix it quickly.

steps.action: Logs into the app and initiates a new service request, selecting 'Plumbing' as the category.
steps.expectedOutcome: A new service request draft is created.

steps.action: Describes the issue in detail ('leaky pipe under kitchen sink'), attaches photos and a short video of the leak.
steps.expectedOutcome: Rich media is attached to the request, providing clear context for service providers.

steps.action: Uses AI to analyze the request and suggests relevant, pre-vetted service providers based on location, skills, and ratings.
steps.expectedOutcome: A list of recommended providers is displayed.

steps.action: Reviews the recommended providers and selects three to receive a bid for the job.
steps.expectedOutcome: The service request is sent to the selected providers.

steps.action: Receives the request, reviews the details, and submits a bid with cost and availability.
steps.expectedOutcome: The bid is sent to the homeowner.

steps.action: Compares the incoming bids, reviews provider profiles, and selects the best one for the job.
steps.expectedOutcome: A service provider is awarded the work, and a work order is generated.

acceptanceCriteria: A homeowner can create a detailed service request with media attachments.
The system recommends qualified service providers.
The homeowner can receive and compare multiple bids.
The homeowner can successfully select a provider and generate a work order.

userJourneys.title: Service Provider Onboards Their Business
userJourneys.scenario: A contracting business wants to join the Hestami network to find new customers. They need to create a profile, define their services, and provide credentials.

steps.action: Registers their business on the platform, providing company name, contact info, and service area.
steps.expectedOutcome: A business account is created.

steps.action: Builds their public profile, adding a business description, logo, and photos of past work.
steps.expectedOutcome: The profile is ready to be viewed by homeowners.

steps.action: Selects the specific services they offer (e.g., 'Drain Cleaning', 'Water Heater Repair') from a predefined list and sets their standard rates.
steps.expectedOutcome: Their service offerings are defined, enabling better job matching.

steps.action: Uploads required credential documents, such as proof of insurance and trade licenses.
steps.expectedOutcome: Documents are submitted for verification.

steps.action: Reviews the submitted credentials and verifies the provider's qualifications.
steps.expectedOutcome: The provider's profile is marked as 'Vetted & Verified'.

steps.action: Adds their technicians as users under their business account.
steps.expectedOutcome: Technician accounts are created and ready to be assigned to jobs.

acceptanceCriteria: A service provider can create a complete business profile.
A provider can upload credentials for verification.
An admin can approve or reject a provider's application.
A provider can manage their technician user accounts.

userJourneys.title: CAM Manages an Architectural Request
userJourneys.scenario: A homeowner in a managed community wants to build a new deck and submits an architectural change request. The CAM needs to manage the review and approval process with the Architectural Review Committee (ARC), which is composed of Board Members.

steps.action: Fills out and submits the architectural change request form via the community portal, attaching blueprints and material specs.
steps.expectedOutcome: The request is submitted and logged in the system with a 'Pending Review' status.

steps.action: Notifies the CAM and the ARC members that a new request has been submitted.
steps.expectedOutcome: Stakeholders are alerted.

steps.action: Reviews the submission for completeness, ensuring all required documents are present. If incomplete, sends it back to the homeowner with a request for more information.
steps.expectedOutcome: The request is validated before being sent to the committee.

steps.action: Logs into the portal, reviews the request details and documents, and adds comments or questions.
steps.expectedOutcome: Committee members can asynchronously review and discuss the request.

steps.action: Casts their vote (Approve, Deny, Approve with Conditions) on the request.
steps.expectedOutcome: The vote is recorded.

steps.action: Once all votes are cast, tallies the result. If approved, automatically updates the request status and generates an official approval letter using a predefined template.
steps.expectedOutcome: The decision is finalized and documented.

steps.action: Notifies the homeowner of the decision and provides them with the official letter.
steps.expectedOutcome: The homeowner is informed of the outcome, completing the process.

acceptanceCriteria: A homeowner can submit an ARC request with all necessary documents.
A CAM can manage the review workflow.
Board members can review, discuss, and vote on the request within the platform.
The system automates the final decision notification and document generation.

userJourneys.title: Technician Completes a Field Service Job
userJourneys.scenario: A technician starts their day and needs to complete a series of scheduled jobs using their mobile device.

steps.action: Logs into the mobile app and views their assigned jobs for the day, displayed on a map with an optimized route.
steps.expectedOutcome: The technician has a clear schedule and efficient travel plan.

steps.action: Selects the first job and taps 'En Route', which notifies the homeowner of their impending arrival.
steps.expectedOutcome: The job status is updated, and the homeowner receives a notification.

steps.action: Arrives at the property, starts a timer to track their time on-site, and reviews the job details and homeowner notes.
steps.expectedOutcome: Accurate time tracking begins, and the technician is fully briefed.

steps.action: Performs the repair, takes 'before' and 'after' photos, and adds notes about the work performed and parts used.
steps.expectedOutcome: A detailed record of the work is captured in the work order.

steps.action: Stops the timer, marks the job as 'Complete', and captures the homeowner's signature on the mobile device.
steps.expectedOutcome: The job is finalized, and proof of completion is recorded.

steps.action: Automatically updates the work order status and notifies the Service Provider's office that the job is ready for invoicing.
steps.expectedOutcome: The back-office is immediately aware the job is complete, accelerating the billing cycle.

acceptanceCriteria: A technician can view their daily schedule on a map.
The technician can update job status in real-time (En Route, In Progress, Complete).
The technician can add photos, notes, and capture a customer signature.
Real-time status changes trigger notifications to homeowners and the back-office.

userJourneys.title: CAM Conducts a Community Inspection and Manages a Violation
userJourneys.scenario: A CAM is performing a routine inspection of the community to ensure compliance with association rules and identifies a property with an issue.

steps.action: Using a tablet, the CAM follows a digital inspection route through the community.
steps.expectedOutcome: The CAM efficiently navigates the community.

steps.action: Identifies a property with a violation (e.g., trash cans left out). At the property's location, they create a new violation record.
steps.expectedOutcome: A new violation case is opened and geo-tagged.

steps.action: Selects the specific rule from the community's governing documents, takes a photo of the violation, and adds a note.
steps.expectedOutcome: The violation is documented with evidence and a clear reference to the rule.

steps.action: Automatically generates a formal notice letter using a pre-approved template, including the photo and a deadline for resolution.
steps.expectedOutcome: A standardized, professional notice is created.

steps.action: Sends the notice to the homeowner via their preferred communication method (email or mail) and logs the communication.
steps.expectedOutcome: The homeowner is officially notified, and an audit trail is created.

steps.action: Receives the notice, resolves the issue (brings in trash cans), and submits a photo through the portal as proof of compliance.
steps.expectedOutcome: The homeowner can easily report resolution.

steps.action: Reviews the homeowner's submission, verifies compliance, and closes the violation case.
steps.expectedOutcome: The violation is resolved and the case is closed.

acceptanceCriteria: A CAM can log violations with photographic evidence from a mobile device.
The system can automatically generate and send violation notices.
Homeowners can submit proof of resolution online.
The entire lifecycle of a violation is tracked in the system.

userJourneys.title: Board Member Reviews Monthly Financial Package
userJourneys.scenario: It's the beginning of the month, and the community's Board of Directors needs to review the financial performance from the previous month to ensure the association is fiscally sound.

steps.action: Automatically closes the books for the previous month and generates a standard financial package (Balance Sheet, Income Statement, AR Aging, etc.).
steps.expectedOutcome: The reporting package is generated without manual intervention.

steps.action: Notifies the CAM and all Board Members that the monthly financial package is ready for review.
steps.expectedOutcome: Board members are prompted to perform their fiduciary duty.

steps.action: Logs into the secure board portal and navigates to the financial reports section.
steps.expectedOutcome: Access is restricted to authorized users.

steps.action: Views the financial dashboard for a high-level overview of key metrics like cash on hand, budget variance, and delinquencies.
steps.expectedOutcome: The board member can quickly assess the community's financial health.

steps.action: Drills down into specific reports, such as the Income Statement, to compare actual expenses against the budget.
steps.expectedOutcome: The board member can investigate and understand financial details.

steps.action: Uses the internal commenting feature to ask the CAM a question about a specific line item, creating a thread for discussion.
steps.expectedOutcome: Collaboration and clarification can happen directly within the platform, maintaining a record of the conversation.

acceptanceCriteria: The system automatically generates a complete financial package each month.
Board members receive timely notifications to review reports.
Board members can access and view all financial reports and a summary dashboard.
Board members can ask questions and discuss financial items within the platform.

userJourneys.title: Homeowner Pays Annual Community Assessment
userJourneys.scenario: It's time for the annual or monthly HOA dues to be paid. The homeowner needs to receive their statement and make a payment.

steps.action: Generates assessment invoices for all properties in the community based on the approved budget.
steps.expectedOutcome: Accurate invoices are created for every homeowner.

steps.action: Delivers the invoices to homeowners based on their selected preference (email with PDF attachment or paper mail).
steps.expectedOutcome: Homeowners receive their statements.

steps.action: Receives the invoice, logs into the resident portal, and navigates to the billing section.
steps.expectedOutcome: The homeowner can see their current balance and payment history.

steps.action: Chooses to make a one-time payment via credit card or ACH.
steps.expectedOutcome: Payment information is securely captured.

steps.action: Alternatively, sets up automatic recurring payments (autopay) so future assessments are paid on time without manual intervention.
steps.expectedOutcome: Future payments are scheduled.

steps.action: Processes the payment, updates the homeowner's account balance, and sends a payment confirmation receipt.
steps.expectedOutcome: The payment is recorded in the general ledger and the homeowner has a receipt.

acceptanceCriteria: The system can generate and distribute assessment invoices to all homeowners.
Homeowners can view their billing history and current balance online.
Homeowners can make secure one-time or recurring payments via ACH or credit card.
Payments are automatically posted to the homeowner's ledger.

userJourneys.title: Hestami Admin Configures a New Community Tenant
userJourneys.scenario: A community management company has signed up to use the Hestami platform for their portfolio of 50 communities. A Hestami Staff member needs to set up their environment.

steps.action: Logs into the system administration panel and creates a new Tenant for the management company.
steps.expectedOutcome: A new, isolated environment is created for the company.

steps.action: Configures the tenant's global settings, such as branding (logo, colors) and default communication templates.
steps.expectedOutcome: The platform is white-labeled for the management company.

steps.action: Sets up the primary admin account for the CAM company.
steps.expectedOutcome: The management company's main user can now access the system.

steps.action: Uses a data import tool to bulk-upload the initial data for the 50 communities, including properties, residents, and opening financial balances.
steps.expectedOutcome: The communities are populated with data, avoiding manual entry.

steps.action: Assigns the relevant communities to the CAM users within the management company.
steps.expectedOutcome: CAMs can only see and manage the communities they are responsible for.

steps.action: Enables or disables specific feature flags for the tenant based on their subscription level.
steps.expectedOutcome: The tenant has access to the correct set of features.

acceptanceCriteria: An admin can create and configure a new, isolated tenant.
Bulk data import tools for properties, residents, and financials are available.
User roles and permissions can be configured for the new tenant.
Feature access can be controlled at the tenant level.

successMetrics: Adoption rate of each pillar by its target user base.
Number of service calls successfully managed through the platform.
Reduction in time and cost for property owners to resolve maintenance issues.
Increased operational efficiency for service providers (e.g., jobs per day per technician).
Automation of manual tasks for community association managers (e.g., percentage of invoices processed by AI).

phasingStrategy.description: Focus on delivering the core value proposition to property owners with the Hestami Home Real Property Assistant.
phasingStrategy.rationale: This phase addresses the primary pain point of property maintenance for the largest user group, creating the foundation for the two-sided marketplace with service providers.

phasingStrategy.description: Build the Hestami Service Provider Field Services Management platform to onboard and serve contractors.
phasingStrategy.rationale: With a base of property owners creating demand, this phase provides the supply side of the marketplace with the tools they need to operate efficiently, creating a flywheel effect.

phasingStrategy.description: Develop the Hestami Community Association Management platform to serve HOAs and property management companies.
phasingStrategy.rationale: This phase expands the platform's reach to the managed community ecosystem, unlocking new revenue streams and creating a more comprehensive real property operating system.

uxRequirements: The platform must provide a seamless and intuitive user experience for all personas, regardless of their technical proficiency.
Mobile-first design for homeowner and technician-facing features.
Dashboards and reporting should be clear, concise, and actionable.
The system should be highly responsive and performant, especially for data-dense displays in the admin portals.

businessDomainProposals.name: Property & Asset Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Core repository for all information related to a physical property, including its structure, assets, documents, and history.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly supports the 'Home Real Property Assistant' pillar and the user journey of onboarding a new property. This is the central hub for the homeowner's assets. Source: user-specified

businessDomainProposals.name: Service & Work Order Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages the full lifecycle of a service request, from initial creation by a homeowner to bidding, work order generation, completion, and invoicing.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Core to the interaction between the Homeowner and Service Provider pillars, as defined in user journeys for creating service calls and managing work orders. Source: user-specified

businessDomainProposals.name: Field Service Operations
businessDomainProposals.description: Handles the operational side for service providers, including scheduling jobs, dispatching technicians, managing routes, and tracking job status in real-time.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Essential for the 'Field Services Management' pillar to provide value to contractors. Supports the journey of managing a work order from dispatch to completion. Source: user-specified

businessDomainProposals.name: Community & Compliance Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Governs the rules and regulations of a community association, including inspections, violation tracking, and enforcement workflows.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly supports the 'Community Association Management' pillar and the user journey for processing a violation. This is a key function for CAMs and Boards. Source: user-specified

businessDomainProposals.name: Financials & Accounting
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages all financial aspects for community associations, including accounts receivable (assessments), accounts payable, general ledger, and financial reporting.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: A critical requirement for the CAM pillar, explicitly mentioned in the requirements for a GL structure and automatic assessments. Source: user-specified

businessDomainProposals.name: AI & Data Intelligence
businessDomainProposals.description: A cross-cutting domain that provides AI-powered services like document analysis, service provider recommendations, and predictive maintenance insights to other domains.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Central to the product's vision of being 'powered by AI'. Explicitly mentioned in requirements for document processing. This domain enables intelligent features across the platform. Source: user-specified
workflowPreview: Suggest Service Providers for a Job

businessDomainProposals.name: User & Identity Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Handles user authentication, authorization, profiles, roles, and permissions across all three pillars of the platform.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: A fundamental requirement for any multi-user system, especially a multi-sided platform like this one with distinct personas and permissions. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Vendor & Provider Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages the network of service providers, including their onboarding, credentialing (insurance, licenses), service offerings, ratings, and performance.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Implicit in the need to connect homeowners with 'trusted' service providers. This domain ensures the quality and reliability of the supply side of the marketplace. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Communications & Notifications
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages all communications sent to users, including emails, SMS, push notifications, and in-app messages. Provides templates and history.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Essential for a platform that coordinates actions between multiple parties (homeowners, vendors, CAMs). Workflows like violation notices and service call updates depend on reliable communication. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Reporting & Analytics
businessDomainProposals.description: Provides dashboards, reports, and data visualizations to help users like Board Members, CAMs, and Service Providers understand performance and make informed decisions.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Addresses the stated pain points of Board Members and CAMs regarding lack of visibility and timely information. A core requirement for any management platform. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Document Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Provides a centralized and secure repository for storing, organizing, versioning, and sharing all documents across the platform.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Extends the simple 'upload' feature into a robust system needed for compliance, history, and AI processing. Many domains (Property, Compliance, Financials) rely on documents. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: Architectural & Change Request Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Manages the process for homeowners to submit architectural or modification requests to the community association, and for the board/committee to review and approve them.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: This is a standard, major workflow in community association management that is currently missing. It's a key part of the 'governance' aspect of the platform. Source: ai-proposed

businessDomainProposals.name: System Administration & Configuration
businessDomainProposals.description: Tools for Hestami Staff to manage the platform itself, including tenant (Community/Service Provider) onboarding, system-wide settings, feature flags, and monitoring.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Required for the 'Hestami Staff / Admin' persona to effectively manage the multi-tenant, multi-pillar platform as described in their goals and pain points. Source: ai-proposed

workflowProposals.name: Service Provider Recommendation
workflowProposals.description: When a homeowner submits a service request, this workflow uses AI to analyze the request and recommend the best-fit service providers from the network.
steps: A service request is created by a homeowner.
The system parses the request description, category, and attached media using natural language processing.
The AI model matches the request's needs against provider profiles, considering factors like skills, service area, past performance ratings, and current availability.
A ranked list of the top 3-5 providers is generated.
The recommended providers are presented to the homeowner.
triggers: Creation of a new service request.

workflowProposals.name: Vendor Credential Expiration Monitoring
workflowProposals.description: This workflow automatically monitors the expiration dates of vendor credentials (like insurance or licenses) and triggers alerts to ensure compliance.
steps: A nightly system job scans all vendor profiles for credentials expiring within the next 60 days.
If an expiring credential is found, a notification is sent to the Service Provider.
Follow-up notifications are sent at 30 days and 7 days before expiration.
If a credential expires, the system automatically flags the provider's profile as 'Non-Compliant' and temporarily suspends them from receiving new job requests.
A notification is sent to the Hestami Admin for review.
Once the provider uploads a new, valid credential, the system lifts the suspension.

workflowProposals.name: Violation Escalation Process
workflowProposals.description: Automates the escalation of unresolved community violations according to the association's predefined enforcement policy.
steps: A violation is created with a resolution deadline.
If the deadline passes and the violation is not marked as resolved, the system checks the community's enforcement policy.
The system automatically escalates the violation to the next stage (e.g., 'Second Notice', 'Final Notice').
A new notice is generated and sent to the homeowner.
If defined in the policy, a fine is automatically added to the homeowner's account ledger.
The workflow repeats until the violation is resolved or reaches the final stage (e.g., 'Sent to Legal').

workflowProposals.name: Automated Accounts Receivable (AR) Collections
workflowProposals.description: Manages the process of collecting delinquent homeowner assessments based on the community's collections policy.
steps: An assessment payment becomes overdue.
The system automatically applies a late fee to the homeowner's account after the grace period ends.
A 'Friendly Reminder' notice is sent to the homeowner.
If payment is not received after a set number of days (e.g., 30 days), a 'Formal Demand' letter is generated and sent.
The workflow continues to escalate based on the policy, potentially notifying the board, or flagging the account for legal action at the final stage.
All communications and fees are logged on the homeowner's account for a complete audit trail.
triggers: An assessment due date passing without payment.

workflowProposals.name: AI Document Analysis and Indexing
workflowProposals.description: When a document is uploaded, this workflow uses AI to process its content, extract key information, and make it searchable.
steps: A user uploads a document (e.g., PDF, JPG) to the system.
The document is sent to an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) service to convert any images of text into machine-readable text.
A Natural Language Processing (NLP) model analyzes the text to identify key entities (e.g., dates, names, invoice amounts, asset model numbers).
The extracted text and entities are added to a search index.
The document is auto-tagged with keywords (e.g., 'invoice', 'HVAC', 'warranty') for easier filtering and organization.
triggers: File upload to the document management system.

workflowProposals.name: Work Order Lifecycle Notifications
workflowProposals.description: Keeps all stakeholders informed about the status of a work order as it progresses from creation to completion.
steps: A work order is created and awarded to a Service Provider.
System notifies the Service Provider of the new job.
The Service Provider schedules the job and assigns a Technician.
System notifies the Homeowner of the scheduled service date.
The Technician marks themselves as 'En Route'.
System sends an 'On the way' notification to the Homeowner with an ETA.
The Technician completes the job.
System notifies the Homeowner and the Service Provider's back-office that the job is complete and ready for invoicing and rating.

integrationProposals.name: Payment Gateway
integrationProposals.description: Processes online payments from homeowners for assessments and invoices. Handles credit card and ACH transactions securely.
integrationProposals.rationale: Required for UJ-8 (Homeowner Pays Annual Community Assessment) and WF-4 (Automated AR Collections) to allow for electronic payments.

integrationProposals.name: Bank Account Synchronization
integrationProposals.description: Connects to community association bank accounts to automatically import transaction data for financial reconciliation within the DOM-ACCOUNTING module.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates the bank reconciliation process, reducing manual data entry for CAMs and ensuring the General Ledger is accurate and up-to-date.

integrationProposals.name: Accounting Software Sync
integrationProposals.description: Enables the export of financial data, including the General Ledger and financial reports, to external accounting platforms.
integrationProposals.rationale: Allows accounting professionals and management companies to continue using their preferred system of record for corporate-level accounting.

integrationProposals.name: Email Delivery Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends all transactional and bulk emails from the platform, including notifications, alerts, reports, and violation notices.
integrationProposals.rationale: Provides a reliable and scalable mechanism for all system communications (DOM-COMMS) and is essential for WF-6 (Work Order Lifecycle Notifications).

integrationProposals.name: SMS Messaging Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends SMS messages for time-sensitive alerts, such as new job assignments for technicians or urgent community announcements.
integrationProposals.rationale: Offers a direct and immediate communication channel for high-priority notifications within DOM-COMMS.

integrationProposals.name: Push Notification Service
integrationProposals.description: Delivers push notifications to the mobile applications used by Homeowners and Technicians.
integrationProposals.rationale: Enables real-time, on-device alerts for UJ-5 (Technician Completes a Field Service Job) and general homeowner notifications.

integrationProposals.name: Cloud File Storage
integrationProposals.description: Provides a secure, scalable, and durable location to store all documents, photos, and other binary files managed by DOM-DOCS.
integrationProposals.rationale: Required to handle the storage and versioning of all documents (ENT-DOCUMENT) uploaded to the system.

integrationProposals.name: Identity Provider (SSO)
integrationProposals.description: Allows users to authenticate using third-party identity providers, such as Google, Microsoft, or enterprise SSO solutions like Okta.
integrationProposals.rationale: Simplifies the login process for all user personas and allows enterprise customers (CAMs, Service Providers) to enforce their own security policies.

integrationProposals.name: AI/ML Platform
integrationProposals.description: Provides the machine learning models and infrastructure for document analysis (OCR, NLP), data extraction, and powering recommendation algorithms.
standardProviders: Google Cloud AI (Vertex AI, Document AI)
integrationProposals.rationale: Core component for WF-5 (AI Document Analysis) and WF-1 (Service Provider Recommendation) within the DOM-AI domain.

integrationProposals.name: Mapping & Geocoding Service
integrationProposals.description: Provides address validation, geocoding (lat/long lookup), map visualization, and route planning capabilities.
integrationProposals.rationale: Essential for DOM-PROPERTY to validate and display property addresses and for DOM-FIELD-OPS to enable route planning for technicians.

integrationProposals.name: Vendor Background Check Service
integrationProposals.description: Integrates with services that perform background checks and verify credentials (licenses, insurance) for service providers.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-2 (Vendor Credential Expiration Monitoring) and enhances trust and safety within the DOM-VENDOR network by automating credential validation.

integrationProposals.name: Public Records API
integrationProposals.description: Connects to databases to pull and validate property details, such as ownership, parcel information, and tax records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates the population of accurate property data during UJ-1 (Homeowner Onboards), reducing manual input and ensuring data integrity for DOM-PROPERTY.

integrationProposals.name: Physical Mail Automation Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends legally required notices and documents via postal mail, such as certified letters for severe violations or collections.
integrationProposals.rationale: Fulfills legal requirements for DOM-COMPLIANCE and DOM-ACCOUNTING where email is not sufficient for formal notices (e.g., WF-3, WF-4).

integrationProposals.name: eSignature Service
integrationProposals.description: Enables legally binding electronic signatures on documents, such as vendor contracts or architectural review approvals.
integrationProposals.rationale: Streamlines formal approval workflows like UJ-4 (CAM Manages an Architectural Request) and vendor onboarding within DOM-ARCH-REVIEW and DOM-VENDOR.

qualityAttributes: The system must enforce strict multi-tenancy to ensure complete data isolation and configuration separation for each Tenant (ENT-TENANT) organization.
Access to all system resources must be governed by a granular Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) model, as defined by User, Role, and Permission entities.
All sensitive data at rest, including documents (DOM-DOCS) and profile information (ENT-PROFILE), must be encrypted using AES-256 or a stronger algorithm.
All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
The platform must comply with relevant data privacy regulations, including GDPR and CCPA, regarding the handling of personal user data.
Any handling of payment card information must adhere to PCI-DSS standards, primarily by delegating processing to a compliant payment gateway.
The platform must be designed for high availability with a target uptime of 99.9%, supported by redundant infrastructure and automated failover.
The architecture must scale horizontally to accommodate growth in users, communities, and data volume without performance degradation.
Mobile application APIs must be optimized for low latency and intermittent connectivity to ensure a responsive experience for field technicians (P-6).
All create, update, and delete operations on critical entities (e.g., ENT-TRANSACTION, ENT-VIOLATION, ENT-USER_ROLE) must be recorded in an immutable audit log (ENT-AUDIT_LOG).
A robust disaster recovery plan must be in place, including regular automated backups and a tested restoration process, to ensure business continuity.
The AI document analysis pipeline (WF-5) must be scalable to handle bulk uploads and return results within a timeframe that does not disrupt user workflows.
The platform must expose a secure, versioned, and well-documented RESTful or GraphQL API to allow for third-party integrations.
All user-facing web and mobile interfaces must adhere to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards.

evidence: The plan contains a clear `productVision`, `summary`, and `productDescription`.
A detailed `personas` section identifies all key user groups, their goals, and pain points.
The scope is well-defined by the three-pillar structure, a `phasingStrategy`, and explicit `constraints`.
System capabilities are clearly outlined in the `requirements`, `userJourneys`, and `businessDomainProposals` sections.
The plan includes extensive `userJourneys` for each key persona and detailed `workflowProposals`.
The plan specifies PostgreSQL and includes comprehensive `entityProposals` with attributes and relationships, forming a strong logical data model.
The plan does not address CI/CD, specific hosting environments, deployment strategies, or operational monitoring.
A dedicated `qualityAttributes` section covers non-functional requirements including availability, scalability, performance, and accessibility.
The `qualityAttributes` section specifies requirements for multi-tenancy, RBAC, encryption, and audit logging.
A dedicated `integrationProposals` section details all necessary external integrations, and the `proposedApproach` defines the internal API strategy.
High-level architecture is well-defined through stack decisions, the `proposedApproach`, and a modular, domain-driven structure.
The implementation plan does not include information on testing strategies, quality assurance, or release management.

classifier_result.rationale: The request explicitly references a specific specification document to be reviewed for implementation readiness.
